items.title:
RT by @WhiteHouse: Today, Jamshid Ghomi, a dual U.S.-Iranian citizen who lives in a $35 million mansion in Orange County, California, was arrested on a federal criminal complaint charging him with selling computer technology to Iranian companies and Iran’s government — including technology to help with Iran’s military and nuclear program.
 
These allegations assert that the defendant violated U.S. sanctions against Iran, aided one of our nation’s enemies, supported Iran’s nuclear program, and got rich doing it. Not only is he being arrested today, but we also are beginning the process of seizing his mansion, which was purchased with his illegal proceeds. Thanks to the work of @USAttyEssayli’s office, @CommerceGov, and @IRS_CI’s Los Angeles Field Office, he will face the full force of justice.
items.summary:

items.title: Christine Lagarde: Women and leadership: widening the pipeline
Fed Beige Book Shows Steady Employment, Higher Inflation
items.summary: Most Fed districts reported higher inflation than in the previous report, driven primarily by the impact of the war in the Middle East on energy prices, according to the US central bank’s Beige Book survey of regional business contacts. Stuart Paul reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Trump’s Iran war drains US oil supplies to lowest level since 2004
items.summary: Industry warns prices could soon jump as inventories reach ‘critically low’ threshold

items.title: Citizen CEO on How AI Is Rebuilding Banking
items.summary: Citizens CEO Bruce Van Saun joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about how the bank transformed the collapse of First Republic into a booming private banking business worth over $16 billion in deposits. He also explains how AI is reshaping banking operations and why he believes consumers, businesses and private credit remain far more resilient than what many investors expect. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Barclays’ Wei Says ‘Prepare for War’ in Software Refinancing
items.summary: Tremors in the software debt market were still top of mind for industry heavyweights speaking at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York on Wednesday.

items.title: Bessent says US inflation jump will be ‘short-term blip’
items.summary: Treasury secretary defends Donald Trump’s economic record amid mounting frustration over Iran war

items.title: Vitol Warns Gasoline Could Be Next Fuel to Face Supply Crunch
items.summary: Gasoline could be the next petroleum product to come under intense supply pressure as the Iran war rumbles on, according to oil trading giant Vitol Group.

items.title: Ares Owed $547 Million After Collapse of Textor’s Eagle Football
items.summary: Ares Management Corp. was owed more than $547 million following the collapse of John Textor’s Eagle Football Group, according to a filing by the administrators on Wednesday.

items.title: Oil Gains as US-Iran Clashes Cloud Peace Deal Outlook
items.summary:
Oil rose for a third day as a fresh exchange of strikes between the US and Iran cast doubt on the prospects of a peace deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
Leslie Palti-Guzman, founder at Energy Vista, gives an update on the outlook for oil and natural gas prices amid the war in Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Xcel Building More Modern, Resilient, Sustainable Grid, Says CEO Frenzel
items.summary:
Xcel Energy CEO Bob Frenzel said that his company is investing across a wide range of energy sources including wind and solar to build a 'more modern, more resilient, more sustainable grid of the future.' 
Talking to Bloomberg Washington Correspondent Tyler Kendall, Frenzel said that despite rising oil prices due to war between the US and Iran, the price of natural gas has seen little fluctuation.

items.title: Iran FM warns any attack on Beirut will trigger  full - scale resumption of war
US House votes to halt Iran war, in rebuke to Trump
items.summary: In the 215-208 vote, four Republicans joined Democrats to pass the measure, which had failed in three previous attempts.

items.title: Gold Holds Drop as US-Iran Clashes Keep Inflation Risks in Focus
items.summary: Gold held a decline after renewed hostilities between the US and Iran jeopardized talks to end the war, keeping energy prices and inflation risks elevated.

items.title: Republican-led House deals blow to Trump over Iran war
items.summary: Lawmakers vote to block president from carrying out more strikes in Middle East without congressional approval
